examples: A single e-scooter visible just below the surface.
A single plastic bottle drifting along the embankment.
Shopping cart half-submerged near the bridge piling.
Unusually milky-grey water near a drainage outflow.
Surface entirely covered in green-blue scum — full bloom.
Dense surface mat covering roughly a third of the visible water.
Patches of greenish water near the houseboat moorings.
Iridescent rainbow sheen across a moderate area near a moored boat.

narrative.intro: Nine approved reports this week, up from zero the previous period. The dominant pattern is a mix of surface issues on the Prinsengracht, alongside some significant blooming activity across several canals.

by_type.trash: Reports from the Prinsengracht, Grimburgwal, and Krom Boomssloot described a mix of small litter like plastic bottles and larger submerged objects such as e-scooters and a shopping cart. These were mostly minor to moderate incidents.
by_type.other: One report from the Prinsengracht noted unusually milky-grey water near a drainage outflow, a concerning observation that warrants attention.
by_type.blooming: Reports from the Prinsengracht, Snoekjesgracht, and Rokin described dense green-blue scum and surface mats, with one case being a severe full bloom.
by_type.petroleum: A single iridescent rainbow sheen was reported on the Prinsengracht, which appears to be a minor incident.

narrative.outlook: Temperatures remain cool, with highs only reaching 15.9 °C and rainfall occurring on several days. These conditions should slow or pause the blooming activity reported on the Prinsengracht, Snoekjesgracht, and Rokin.
narrative.thanks: Thank you to the nine citizens who shared their photos this week. Your vigilance helps us see what's happening in the water. To everyone reading: if you spot something unusual, please send us your next report. 💧
